en: Let all who are under a yoke as slaves regard their own masters as worthy of all honor, so that the name of God and the teaching may not be reviled.
zh: 凡負軛作奴僕的，應當看自己的主人是配受十分的敬重，免得 神的名和道理被人褻瀆。

en: Those who have believing masters must not be disrespectful on the ground that they are brothers; rather they must serve all the better since those who benefit by their good service are believers and beloved. Teach and urge these things.
zh: 奴僕有信主的主人，不可因為他們是弟兄而輕看他們；倒要加意服事他們，因為這些受到服事的益處的，是信主蒙愛的人。 你要把這些事教導人，勸勉人。

en: If anyone teaches a different doctrine and does not agree with the sound words of our Lord Jesus Christ and the teaching that accords with godliness,
zh: 如果有人傳別的教義，不接受我們的主耶穌基督純正的話語，和那敬虔的道理，

en: he is puffed up with conceit and understands nothing. He has an unhealthy craving for controversy and for quarrels about words, which produce envy, dissension, slander, evil suspicions,
zh: 他是自高自大，一無所知，反而專好問難爭辯，由此產生妒忌、紛爭、毀謗、惡意的猜疑，

en: and constant friction among people who are depraved in mind and deprived of the truth, imagining that godliness is a means of gain.
zh: 就和那些心術敗壞，喪失真理的人不斷地爭吵。他們視敬虔為得利的門路。

en: Now there is great gain in godliness with contentment,
zh: 其實敬虔而又知足，就是得大利的途徑，

en: for we brought nothing into the world, and we cannot take anything out of the world.
zh: 因為我們沒有帶甚麼到世上來，也不能帶甚麼去。

en: But if we have food and clothing, with these we will be content.
zh: 只要有衣有食，就應當知足。

en: But those who desire to be rich fall into temptation, into a snare, into many senseless and harmful desires that plunge people into ruin and destruction.
zh: 但那些想要發財的人，就落在試探中和陷阱裡；又落在許多無知而有害的私慾裡，使人沉淪在敗壞和滅亡中；

en: For the love of money is a root of all kinds of evils. It is through this craving that some have wandered away from the faith and pierced themselves with many pangs.
zh: 因為貪財是萬惡之根。有人貪愛錢財，就被引誘離開真道，用許多痛苦把自己刺透了。

en: But as for you, O man of God, flee these things. Pursue righteousness, godliness, faith, love, steadfastness, gentleness.
zh: 但你這屬 神的人啊，應該逃避這些事，要追求公義、敬虔、信心、愛心、忍耐和溫柔。

en: Fight the good fight of the faith. Take hold of the eternal life to which you were called and about which you made the good confession in the presence of many witnesses.
zh: 要為信仰打那美好的仗，持定永生；你是為這永生而蒙召的，又在許多的證人面前承認過美好的信仰。

en: I charge you in the presence of God, who gives life to all things, and of Christ Jesus, who in his testimony before Pontius Pilate made the good confession,
zh: 我在賜生命給萬物的 神面前，並那在本丟．彼拉多面前見證過美好的信仰的基督耶穌面前囑咐你，

en: to keep the commandment unstained and free from reproach until the appearing of our Lord Jesus Christ,
zh: 你當毫無玷污，無可指摘地持守這命令，直到我們主耶穌基督的顯現。

en: which he will display at the proper time- he who is the blessed and only Sovereign, the King of kings and Lord of lords,
zh: 到了適當的時候，那可稱頌的、獨一的全能者，萬王之王，萬主之主，必把基督的顯現表明出來。

en: who alone has immortality, who dwells in unapproachable light, whom no one has ever seen or can see. To him be honor and eternal dominion. Amen.
zh: 只有他永遠不死，住在不能接近的光裡，沒有人見過他，人也不能看見他。願尊榮和永遠的權能都歸給他。阿們。

en: As for the rich in this present age, charge them not to be haughty, nor to set their hopes on the uncertainty of riches, but on God, who richly provides us with everything to enjoy.
zh: 你要囑咐那些今世富有的人，叫他們不要心高氣傲，也不要寄望在浮動的財富上，卻要仰望那厚賜百物給我們享用的 神。

en: They are to do good, to be rich in good works, to be generous and ready to share,
zh: 又要囑咐他們行善，在善事上富足，慷慨好施。

en: thus storing up treasure for themselves as a good foundation for the future, so that they may take hold of that which is truly life.
zh: 這樣，就為自己在來世積聚財富，作美好的基礎，好叫他們能夠得著那真正的生命。

en: O Timothy, guard the deposit entrusted to you. Avoid the irreverent babble and contradictions of what is falsely called "knowledge,"
zh: 提摩太啊，你要保守所交託你的，避免世俗的空談和冒稱是知識的那種反調；

en: for by professing it some have swerved from the faith. Grace be with you.
zh: 有些人自稱有這知識，就偏離了真道。願恩惠與你們同在。
